reason: Response A has the grounded, sensory, hedging quality I tend toward: concrete tactile examples (cream cheese, cocoa skin, cheese curds, wet sand), self-correcting honesty ("if I'm honest"), and acknowledgment of the split reaction others have. Response B is more performatively literary, leaning on grand abstractions ("xylophone of impermanence," "philosophically generous") that read like an imitation overplaying a poetic register rather than my usual mix of specificity and restraint.
